extracted_quotes.quote: Every time I saw buzzards circling overhead, Dad would say that he recognized Buster among them and that he was coming back to thank us.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: The Hot Pot was a natural sulfur spring in the desert north of town, surrounded by craggy rocks and quicksand.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: The water was warm to the touch and smelled like rotten eggs.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: It was so full of minerals that rough, chalky encrustations had built up along the edges, like a coral reef.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: A couple of children and wild teenagers had drowned there, and people said the Owl Club said when their bodies floated back to the surface, they’d been literally boiled.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: Large bodies of water scared me. They seemed unnatural—oddities in the deserted towns where we’d lived.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: I waded in up to my shoulders. The water around my chest was warm, and the rocks I was standing on felt so hot I wanted to keep moving.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: the hot, smelly water

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: The clouds above the setting sun were streaked scarlet and purple.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: The temperature was dropping quickly, from searing hot to chilly within a matter of minutes, like it always did in the desert at dusk.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: we had the desert

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: thick black rubber smoke that made our noses sting as it rolled past us into the air

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: a clump of sagebrush

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: Its red paint had been bleached by the desert sun and had turned orange along the rusting trim.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: I ran outside with a paper bag to gather up my favorite rocks.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: we were out in the desert

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: GRANDMA SMITH'S big white house had green shutters and was surrounded by eucalyptus trees.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: The front yard had a palm tree, and the backyard had orange trees that grew real oranges.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: I particularly loved the palm tree, which made me think I had arrived at some kind of oasis.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: There were also hollyhocks and oleander bushes with pink and white flowers.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: surrounded by palm trees and banana trees

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: the playground at Emerson was covered with lush green grass

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: You see that tree over there?

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: I can see the individual leaves on it.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: she pointed out starlings perched on the telephone wires.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: the way the setting sun painted the underbellies of the clouds pink but left the piled-up tops purple

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: woodland animals

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: rural landscapes

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: urban landscapes

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: flowers, fruits and vegetables

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: Mountain Goat, I still feel bad about making you leave your rock collection back in Battle Mountain

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: about how the sun shone all the time and how it was so warm that we ran around barefoot even in the dead of winter, about how we ate lettuce in the farm fields and picked carloads of grapes and slept on blankets under the stars.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: she had blue eyes the color of the ocean that bordered California’s beaches.

extracted_quotes.author: JwannetteWalls
extracted_quotes.quote: I walked around the mountain to apply for the job.
